examinerNotes: Strong overall performance with well-diversified portfolio. Crops concentration at 45% approaching internal policy limit of 50% — management has acknowledged and is actively monitoring. Credit administration practices are sound with timely risk rating migration. Capital levels well above regulatory minimums providing significant cushion for stress scenarios. Recommend continued annual exam cycle.

Well-managed institution with experienced leadership team and strong governance culture. Board engagement notably high with active risk committee. Earnings adequate but margin compression from competitive pricing pressure noted. Management proactively addressing through fee income initiatives and operating efficiency improvements. No material examination findings.

Elevated risk profile driven by deteriorating asset quality in livestock and dairy segments. Non-accrual loans at 4.8% of total portfolio, significantly above peer average of 2.1%. Three large borrower relationships in workout status representing 8% of capital. Management has implemented enhanced monitoring but corrective action plan timelines have slipped. Capital adequacy weakening — board approved capital conservation measures including dividend reduction. Moved to semi-annual exam cycle. Probation status warranted until measurable improvement demonstrated.

Insurance operations performing well with strong loss ratios and growing policy count. Earnings driven by favorable crop conditions and premium rate adequacy. Reinsurance program appropriately structured. Technology platform modernization project on track and within budget. Staffing levels adequate with low turnover. No material concerns.

Newly chartered association in early operational stage. Initial loan portfolio growing rapidly with adequate underwriting standards but limited seasoning to assess credit quality. Management team experienced but building institutional processes. Liquidity dependent on Central Farm Bank line — diversification plan needed. Capital adequate for current volume but growth trajectory requires close monitoring of capital consumption. Semi-annual exam cycle appropriate for new charter status.
